info.name: Hawiya AI — WizSM integration
info.description:
Endpoints WizSM needs to integrate against Hawiya AI for the Diwan deployment.

**Scope.** Hawiya AI extracts structured identity data from passport scans. WizSM runs its own duplicate-check against the Diwan registry, so this collection deliberately omits Hawiya's `/v1/identity/resolve` and `/v1/persons/*` endpoints — those are for consumers without their own registry.

**Endpoints**

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|
| GET | `/v1/health` | Liveness — wire to your monitoring |
| GET | `/v1/ready` | Readiness — checks Postgres reachability |
| POST | `/v1/documents/extract` | Extract structured fields from a passport image |

**Auth (Phase 1).** Bearer token + `X-Tenant-ID` header. The bearer is a stub for the pilot; production will replace with mTLS or OAuth2 — Hawiya will tell WizSM which before go-live.

**Required headers on every authenticated request:**
- `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
- `X-Tenant-ID: <uuid>` — your tenant id from the Hawiya team
- `X-Request-ID: <uuid>` (optional; generated server-side if absent and echoed in the response)
- `Idempotency-Key: <opaque>` (optional but recommended for `POST` retries)

**Rate limit.** 100 requests per minute per tenant on `/v1/documents/extract`. Configurable per tenant. 429 with `Retry-After` header when exceeded.

**Errors.** Every non-2xx response uses the same envelope:
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "DOCUMENT_UNREADABLE",
    "message": "...",
    "details": { },
    "trace_id": "..."
  }
}
```

Common codes you'll see: `UNAUTHENTICATED`, `TENANT_REQUIRED`, `TENANT_INVALID`, `EMPTY_UPLOAD`, `PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE`, `UNSUPPORTED_DOCUMENT`, `DOCUMENT_UNREADABLE`, `OCR_UNAVAILABLE`, `RATE_LIMITED`, `IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_CONFLICT`, `INTERNAL_ERROR`.

**Setup.** Use the `Hawiya AI — Dev` environment in this folder. Set `tenant_id` to the UUID Hawiya provides; leave `bearer_token` as `dev` for the pilot.

variable.description: Override per environment (use the Postman environment file rather than editing this).
Tenant UUID assigned by the Hawiya AI team.
Bearer token. Phase 1 stub; production will use mTLS or OAuth2.

item.name: Health
item.description: Liveness + readiness checks. No auth required.

request.description:
Cheap liveness probe — returns 200 if the process is up. Wire this to your monitoring (1-second interval is fine).

**No auth required.** Safe to call from anywhere on the network.

**Response (200):**
```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "version": "0.1.0"
}
```

item.name: Document extraction
item.description: The endpoint WizSM calls for every passport scan.

header.description: Recommended. Same key + same body within 24h returns the cached response. Same key + different body returns 422 IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_CONFLICT.

formdata.description: Passport image — JPEG, PNG, TIFF, or PDF. Max 10 MB. Pick a file when sending.
Optional. WizSM's own correlation id; echoed in audit_log so cross-system traceability works.

request.description:
**The endpoint WizSM calls for every passport scan.**

Upload the passport image as `file` in a `multipart/form-data` body. Hawiya AI runs PassportEye + Tesseract over the MRZ region, validates all five ICAO 9303 checksums, and returns structured fields.

**Required:**
- `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
- `X-Tenant-ID: <uuid>`
- `file` (multipart) — passport image

**Optional:**
- `consumer_request_id` (multipart text) — your correlation id, echoed in audit log
- `Idempotency-Key` header — recommended on retries

**Response (200):**
```json
{
  "extraction_id": "uuid",
  "document_type": "passport",
  "fields": {
    "document_type": "P",
    "issuing_country": "ARE",
    "document_number": "P1234567",
    "surname": "ALMANSOORI",
    "given_names": "MOHAMED",
    "nationality": "ARE",
    "date_of_birth": "1990-01-12",
    "sex": "M",
    "date_of_expiry": "2030-01-01",
    "personal_number": null
  },
  "confidence_per_field": {
    "document_number": 0.99,
    "date_of_birth": 0.99,
    "date_of_expiry": 0.99,
    "surname": 0.85,
    "given_names": 0.85,
    "sex": 0.95
  },
  "checksum_status": "all_pass",
  "processing_path": "mrz_only",
  "processing_time_ms": 287
}
```

**Error codes** (from the shared error envelope):
- `400 EMPTY_UPLOAD` — file is empty
- `413 PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE` — file > 10 MB
- `415 UNSUPPORTED_DOCUMENT` — bytes don't look like JPEG/PNG/TIFF/PDF
- `422 DOCUMENT_UNREADABLE` — OCR couldn't find or parse the MRZ
- `503 OCR_UNAVAILABLE` — Tesseract backend down — retry with backoff

exec: pm.test('200 OK with extraction id', function () {

formdata.description: Send any non-image file to see the 415 error envelope.

request.description: Trigger the `415 UNSUPPORTED_DOCUMENT` path. Useful for verifying your error-handling code without crafting a bad image.
Trigger the `401 TENANT_REQUIRED` path by omitting the `X-Tenant-ID` header. Useful for verifying that your client surfaces auth errors clearly to operators.
